zh.lifeSummary: 當Ieuan ap Rhys Gryg , de Deuheubarth於1195年出生在英國威爾斯布雷康史爾利安費韓爾 -度時，他的父親Prince Rhys Gryg30歲，母親Ellwyn verch Trahaearn46歲。他和Jonet de Mortimer至少有1個兒子和3個女兒。

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Long, Minh, Muoi, Phan, Phat, Vinh, Cuong, Hien, Hoa, Lam, Lan.  Chinese Wing, Chi, Kin, Cheuk, Chi Wai, Chuen, Hon, Keung, Wah, Wai, Chiu, Ho.
Chinese: alternative Mandarin form of the surname 敖, see   Ao  .
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Auerbach
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Moshe, Emanuel, Isidor, Bronia, Chaim, Faigy, Menachem, Meyer, Yechezkel.
German: habitational name from any of several places in southern Germany and Austria called Auerbach ‘the stream at the water meadow’. See also 2,   Au  , and   Bach  .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from the town of Auerbach in the Upper Palatinate, Bavaria. Compare   Averbach  .

variants.name: Ao
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 敖, meaning both ‘rambling’ and ‘deposed king without posthumous titles’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the personal name of Tai Ao (太敖), teacher of the legendary king Zhuanxu (traditional dates: 2514–2437 BC ). (ii) it is said that the surname is also borne by descendants of deposed kings of the former state of Chu, with no posthumous titles, during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

variants.name: Auer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Alois, Hans, Erwin, Matthias, Siegfried, Bernd, Bernhard, Erna, Franz, Fritz, Hermann, Horst.

German (mainly Bavaria): topographic name for someone living by a water meadow, German Au, or a habitational name for someone from a place called Au or Aue (see Au and Aue ). This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Czechia, and Slovenia.

nameMeanings.name: Rhys
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Rees
meanings: German: habitational name from a place in the Rhineland called Rees.
German: from a short form of the personal name   Andreas  .

nameMeanings.name: Gryg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Gregg
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): from the Middle English personal name Greg, a pet form of Gregory ; compare Grigg .

nameMeanings.name: Ieuan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When Ieuan ap Rhys Gryg , de Deuheubarth was born in 1195, in Llanfihangel Cwm-du, Breconshire, Wales, United Kingdom, his father, Prince Rhys Gryg, was 30 and his mother, Ellwyn verch Trahaearn, was 46. He had at least 1 son and 3 daughters with Jonet de Mortimer.

ko.lifeSummary: Ieuan ap Rhys Gryg , de Deuheubarth(이)가 1195년 영국 웨일스 브레콘실르 랜피핸걸 cw-드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Prince Rhys Gryg(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Ellwyn verch Trahaearn(은)는 46세였다. 그는 Jonet de Mortimer(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ieuan ap Rhys Gryg , de Deuheubarth родился в 1195 г. в местности лланфихангел квм-ду, брекуншир, Уэльс, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Prince Rhys Gryg было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Ellwyn verch Trahaearn, 46.
